title: PostgreSQL 18 Domain Schema, Keys, Constraints, and Index Intent
description: The database uses URLs as mutable primary keys, repeats publisher strings, permits cross-tenant references, lacks temporal constraints, and indexes without query evidence. Learners cumulatively produce constrained relational feed-service schema through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model tenants, users, sources, feeds, subscriptions, entries, authors, categories, content versions, fetches, and reader state with clear ownership.
Use stable internal keys plus scoped external identities and preserve canonical URL changes without breaking references.
Encode nullability, checks, unique scopes, foreign keys, cascades, timestamps, states, and length budgets in PostgreSQL.
